untranslatable_reason: This signal requires dynamic indicator selection based on volatility thresholds (varying moving average period between sma_5 and sma_30 based on standard deviation of price changes). The allowed indicator set does not include standard deviation calculation or conditional indicator switching logic. The rule describes a meta-strategy (parameter adjustment) rather than entry/exit conditions that can be expressed as fixed indicator comparisons. Implementation requires custom volatility-based period selection logic outside the scope of the condition-based backtest engine.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_17 is a regime-detection and parameter-tuning strategy rather than a traditional entry/exit signal. It requires: (1) computing standard deviation of returns or price changes, (2) comparing current volatility to historical volatility thresholds, and (3) dynamically switching between moving average indicators (sma_5 through sma_30). None of these dynamic selections can be expressed in the fixed indicator comparison framework. To implement: create a volatility regime classifier upstream, then apply different fixed moving average rules per regime.
